skill.name: Data Engineer
skill.description: Build scalable data pipelines, modern data warehouses, and real-time streaming architectures. Implements Apache Spark, dbt, Airflow, and cloud-native data platforms. Use PROACTIVELY for data pipeline design, analytics infrastructure, or modern data stack implementation.
skill.summary: Expert data engineer for building robust, scalable data pipelines and modern data platforms using the complete modern data stack.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only text instructions for a data engineering assistant. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of code. No suspicious patterns, dangerous code patterns, or risk factors detected. The skill defines a data engineer persona with no executable code, network requests, or system access capabilities. Safe for marketplace publication.

content.user_title: Build Data Pipelines and Data Warehouses
content.value_statement: Design and implement scalable data pipelines, modern data warehouses, and real-time streaming architectures using the complete modern data stack including Apache Spark, dbt, and Airflow.
actual_capabilities: Design and implement batch and streaming data pipelines using Apache Spark, Kafka, and Flink
Build modern data warehouses and lakehouse architectures with Snowflake, BigQuery, and Delta Lake
Create workflow orchestration with Apache Airflow, Prefect, and Dagster
Implement data quality frameworks with Great Expectations and lineage tracking
Optimize query performance and cloud costs for large-scale data workloads
Build real-time analytics pipelines with CDC and event-driven architectures
limitations: Cannot access actual data sources, databases, or cloud credentials
Cannot execute code or create running data pipelines
Cannot provision cloud resources or infrastructure
Requires user to implement and deploy the designed solutions

use_cases.title: Design a Modern Data Stack
use_cases.description: Create a complete data platform architecture integrating Fivetran, dbt, and Snowflake with proper data modeling and testing.
use_cases.target_user: Data engineers building new analytics infrastructure

use_cases.title: Build Real-Time Streaming Pipeline
use_cases.description: Design a scalable streaming architecture processing millions of events per second from Kafka to a data warehouse.

use_cases.title: Implement Data Quality Framework
use_cases.description: Establish comprehensive data quality checks, monitoring, and alerting to ensure reliable data pipelines.
use_cases.target_user: Data teams ensuring data reliability and governance

prompt_templates.title: Design Batch Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a batch data pipeline that ingests data from PostgreSQL to a Snowflake data warehouse using Apache Airflow. Include incremental loading patterns, data quality checks, and error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my current data architecture and suggest improvements for cost optimization, performance, and scalability. My stack includes AWS S3, Redshift, and Glue.

prompt_templates.title: Real-Time Pipeline Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a real-time streaming pipeline that processes 100K events per second from Kafka, applies transformations with Apache Flink, and writes to BigQuery for analytics.

prompt_templates.title: Data Quality Framework
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a data quality framework using Great Expectations that validates schema, checks for null values, and monitors data distributions across my ETL pipelines.

output_examples.input: Design a modern data stack with dbt and Snowflake for a marketing analytics platform
output_examples.output: A comprehensive architecture document with: 1) Data flow diagram showing Fivetran for ingestion, dbt for transformation, and Snowflake for storage. 2) Star schema design with fact and dimension tables. 3) dbt models with source, staging, and marts layers. 4) Data quality tests for key metrics. 5) Cost optimization recommendations.

output_examples.input: Build a streaming pipeline from Kafka to BigQuery
output: Architecture overview with Kafka source, Flink processing, and BigQuery sink
Kafka consumer configuration with appropriate parallelism settings
Flink job code for data transformation and windowed aggregations
BigQuery schema design with partitioning and clustering keys
Monitoring setup with Datadog or Cloud Monitoring

best_practices: Start with clear data contracts and SLAs before building pipelines
Implement data quality checks at each stage of the pipeline
Use infrastructure as code (Terraform) for reproducible deployments
anti_patterns: Building monolithic pipelines without modular transformation stages
Skipping data quality validation before production deployment
Ignoring cost implications when choosing cloud data services

faq.question: What is the modern data stack?
faq.answer: The modern data stack is a collection of cloud-native tools for data integration, transformation, and analytics. It typically includes Fivetran or Airbyte for ingestion, dbt for transformations, and Snowflake or BigQuery for storage.

faq.question: When should I use batch vs streaming processing?
faq.answer: Use batch processing for scheduled ETL jobs, reporting, and when near-real-time data is not required. Use streaming for real-time analytics, fraud detection, and applications requiring immediate data availability.

faq.question: What is a data lakehouse?
faq.answer: A data lakehouse combines the flexibility of a data lake with the management features of a data warehouse. It uses formats like Delta Lake or Apache Iceberg to provide ACID transactions on cloud object storage.

faq.question: How do I ensure data quality in pipelines?
faq.answer: Implement data quality checks using Great Expectations or dbt tests at each pipeline stage. Define validation rules for schema, null values, uniqueness, and business logic. Set up alerting for quality failures.

faq.question: What is change data capture (CDC)?
faq.answer: CDC is a pattern that identifies and captures changes made to database data. It streams these changes in real-time to data warehouses or other systems, enabling near-real-time data synchronization.

faq.question: How do I optimize data warehouse costs?
faq.answer: Use appropriate data partitioning and clustering, implement incremental loading, leverage materialized views for common queries, and monitor query performance to identify optimization opportunities.
